title: Ordinary Markdown is displayed as literal source
summary: Markdown files retain every source delimiter and destination on screen; syntax colors may aid reading, but concealment, rendered substitutions, and automatic save-time formatting are disabled.
details: Triple-backtick and tilde code fences remain visible, including their language identifier.
Emphasis, strong, strikeout, link, checkbox, heading, and inline-code markup remain spelled exactly as stored rather than being hidden or replaced with presentation glyphs.

The global default conceallevel is zero. Markdown syntax conceal is disabled before syntax initialization, and Markdown FileType plus BufWinEnter transitions reassert the zero window-local conceal level.
Markdown remains syntax highlighted and fenced-language injection remains available; color does not change character identity.
Conform does not format Markdown on save. Explicit future authoring tools must remain separate from passive presentation.
This decision concerns ordinary .md/.markdown/.mdx files. The separately accepted frontend rendering of triple-slash documentation blocks in C++ headers is unchanged.
rationale: Hidden fence boundaries made document structure unreadable and made it difficult to distinguish literal Markdown from its rendered interpretation.
A source editor should expose the bytes that will be reviewed, versioned, and consumed by downstream Markdown processors.
Disabling both syntax conceal and the window option avoids a fragile fix that works only until a window carrying another buffer's conceal state is reused.
alternatives: Hide markup only away from the cursor; rejected because document structure would still change during navigation.
Keep an Obsidian/render-markdown presentation toggle; rejected because ordinary Markdown should have one predictable literal view and no such renderer is active in the current configuration.
Disable all Markdown syntax highlighting; rejected because colors do not obscure source characters and remain useful.

provenance.note: Owner reported hidden triple-backtick fences as unreadable and requested no presentation adjustments in Markdown files.
The active cause was a stale global conceallevel=2 retained after obsidian.nvim had been removed; no current Markdown rendering plugin existed.

evidence.claim: A real Markdown FileType/BufWinEnter transition resets an inherited conceal window, disables syntax conceal, shows both triple-backtick fence rows on Neovim's screen grid, retains source lines exactly, and leaves the buffer unchanged.

questions: Why is conceallevel zero in Markdown?
May Markdown emphasis or link syntax be replaced visually?
Does the Markdown rule disable C++ triple-slash documentation rendering?

last_verified.method: Owner decision, stale-option source audit, and isolated Markdown window-transition contract.
